pattern: Digit reversal is only lossless when there is no trailing zero to swallow, so the whole problem collapses to one divisibility check.
hints: Try reversing a few numbers by hand and notice which ones come back different after the second reversal.
A reversal drops any leading zero that appears in the reversed string, so information is lost exactly when the original number ends in a zero digit.
The answer is true whenever the number is zero itself, or whenever its last digit is not zero; otherwise it is false — no actual string reversal is needed.

edge_inputs.description: zero is its own reversal both times
one trailing zero loses information on reversal
multiple trailing zeros still lose information
maximum allowed value ends in zero
largest value just under the maximum with no trailing zero
interior zero digit but no trailing zero is still safe
value with an internal repeated digit and no trailing zero

stress.description: uniformly sampled values across the full allowed range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    hi = min(size, 1000000)
    num = rng.randrange(0, hi + 1)
    return [num]
